prompt: Construct a RoadRunner Dispatch & Commerce Console supporting an offline-first field operation where an Administrator configures products, orders, and workforce rules, a Dispatcher manages task assignment, a Worker accepts and completes jobs, and a Compliance Reviewer audits employer/job content inside the same app. The Android UI provides a fast catalog-to-cart flow with cart merge across sessions (same customer + store merges automatically; conflicting prices trigger a review banner), checkout with shipping templates (Standard 3–5 business days, Expedited 1–2 business days, Local Pickup), stacked discount validation (max 3 discounts, percent-off cannot exceed 40% total, and discounts never apply to sales tax), invoice details, and required order notes for regulated items. At checkout, the app runs amount consistency checks (items + discounts + tax + shipping must equal the displayed total within $0.01) and anti-tampering rules by locking calculated fields and showing a “Recalculate” warning if line items are edited after totals are computed. Dispatchers can run task matching in either grab-order or assigned mode using configurable weights for time window adherence, current workload, and worker reputation; to keep offline and avoid GPS/map services, “distance” is handled as a dispatcher-entered zone score (e.g., Zone A=1 through Zone E=5). Employer onboarding and content compliance are handled locally: employers are verified by required fields (legal name, EIN format check, US address format check) and all job/order notes pass a sensitive-word ruleset; users can file reports with attached evidence retained on-device, and violations trigger takedown, account suspension (7/30/365-day presets), or traffic throttling (limit visibility to internal users only) with an anti-harassment policy requiring two warnings before suspension unless content includes zero-tolerance terms. All data persists on-device using Room (SQLite) with encrypted-at-rest storage for credentials, employer identities, and report evidence; passwords are salted and hashed (minimum 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes). Core entities include Product, Cart, Order, ShippingTemplate, DiscountRule, Employer, ComplianceCase/AuditLog, Worker, Task, Zone, and ReputationEvent, with composite indexes such as (orgId, status, updatedAt) for orders/tasks and (brand, series, model) for catalog search; critical operations (checkout finalization, task acceptance) must run in transactions to prevent partial writes. Lists render via RecyclerView + DiffUtil and should maintain 60fps with 10,000-row datasets; catalog media uses downsampling plus an LRU cache to keep incremental image memory under 20MB and avoid OOM. All database and media IO runs off the main thread (ViewModel + UseCase with background executors) to prevent ANR, and conflict controls prevent duplicate acceptance by enforcing a unique constraint on (taskId, acceptedBy) and a 3-second local mutex during acceptance. Screen layouts use ConstraintLayout for phones and tablets, and Android 10+ scoped storage is respected for imports/exports via system document picker with local file format validation and SHA-256 fingerprints. Despite legacy references to Spring Boot + Thymeleaf + MySQL, this project must ship as a standalone Java Android APK with no server, no web UI, and no remote database dependencies.
